Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an enabling upstream entry gene for reconstruction of cinchona alkaloid biosynthesis in heterologous hosts; should be stacked with STR, DCS, DCE, MAT, MCC, CiS, CiO and optionally T5H/OMT1/STTr for methoxylated products. Use a high-activity orthologue if native activity is limiting.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables strictosidine deglycosylation and supports flux from strictosidine into downstream cinchona alkaloid scaffold formation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana using the Catharanthus roseus orthologue CrSGD; comparison of product formation from strictosidine in reconstructed pathway.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaf discs with exogenous strictosidine or 10-methoxystrictosidine feeding.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: CpSGD1/2/3 sequences reported, but expression pattern in C. pubescens not detailed in the excerpt.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: CrSGD from Catharanthus roseus used as a high-activity orthologue for reconstitution; tissue pattern not reported here.
Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: CpSGD1: PX842834; CpSGD2: PX842835; CpSGD3: PX842836
Common_Genes.Gene_Name: SGD (strictosidine beta-glucosidase; CpSGD1/CpSGD2/CpSGD3; CrSGD used in reconstitution)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Bulk RNA-seq and snRNA-seq for C. pubescens: BioProject PRJNA1347772
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional characterization in this paper emphasized the C. roseus orthologue CrSGD because it showed high catalytic activity. CpSGD paralog sequences were deposited but native CpSGD functional comparison is not detailed in the provided text.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct tradeoff reported; pathway engineering may require balancing with downstream enzymes to avoid intermediate accumulation.
Common_Genes.Source_Species: Cinchona pubescens / Catharanthus roseus orthologue used for reconstruction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SGD is required to convert strictosidine into the reactive aglycone entry point for downstream cinchona alkaloid biosynthesis. 2. A high-activity CrSGD orthologue enabled efficient reconstitution of downstream ketone quinoline products in N. benthamiana. 3. Native CpSGD paralogs were sequence-deposited but not the main focus of validation in this study.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: quinine; quinidine; cinchonidine; cinchonine and dihydro congeners
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cinchona tissues, especially root and stem for late-stage pathway expression; alkaloids also present in leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimalarial drug activity (quinine); antiarrhythmic activity (quinidine); chiral catalyst utility (cinchonidine/cinchonine)

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cinchona alkaloid biosynthesis / monoterpene indole alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Entry-point enzyme for establishing flux from tryptamine and secologanin into cinchona alkaloid biosynthesis; should be stacked with SGD, DCS, DCE, MAT, MCC, CiS, CiO and optional T5H/OMT1/STTr for methoxylated products and substrate diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses tryptamine and secologanin to form strictosidine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the first committed monoterpene indole alkaloid intermediate feeding cinchona alkaloid production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional assignment and used in pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana with exogenous/native substrates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the initial committed precursor pool for target product biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; substrate feeding with tryptamine analogues and secologanin.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with upstream pathway genes; enriched with other pathway genes in leaf epidermal clusters 4-6 based on snRNA-seq context.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: C. pubescens bulk RNA-seq and snRNA-seq: PRJNA1347772
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously known upstream enzyme; not newly discovered here but part of the reconstructed pathway and coexpression analyses.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core monoterpene indole alkaloid entry step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed-step enzyme; key upstream entry point
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STR supplies strictosidine, the universal monoterpene indole alkaloid precursor for cinchona alkaloids. 2. Its coexpression and cell-type enrichment matched newly identified cinchona pathway genes. 3. Inclusion of STR in N. benthamiana enabled production of downstream cinchona alkaloid intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cinchona tissues including leaves, stems and roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal and catalytic activities of cinchona alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required in heterologous stacks to convert strictosidine aglycone toward corynantheal intermediates; should be paired with DCE and downstream scaffold-forming enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the deglycosylated strictosidine-derived intermediate to form dihydrocorynantheine aldehyde/dihydrocorynantheal branch intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports formation of corynantheal/dihydrocorynantheal precursors needed for cinchona alkaloid scaffold assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior functional assignment referenced; employed in pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate upstream precursor for corynantheal scaffold.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: N. benthamiana reconstruction and leaf disc assays.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with STR, DCE and MAT; enriched in leaf epidermal clusters 4-6.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: STR; DCE; KR1; KR2; KR3; KR4; KR5; KR6; KR7; KR8
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: C. pubescens bulk RNA-seq and snRNA-seq: PRJNA1347772
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as coexpression bait to identify reductases and transferases.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DCS functions in the early conversion from strictosidine aglycone toward corynantheal scaffolds. 2. It coexpresses with downstream cinchona alkaloid genes and helped guide candidate discovery. 3. It was necessary for successful heterologous pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal and catalytic roles of cinchona alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary partner with DCS to generate corynantheal-derived scaffold precursors in engineered hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolytic/esterase step acting with DCS to form dihydrocorynantheal from strictosidine-derived intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables production of corynantheal-related intermediates required for downstream quinuclidine and quinoline scaffold formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior functional assignment referenced; included in N. benthamiana pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate upstream precursor for target alkaloid scaffold biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with STR, DCS and MAT; enriched in leaf epidermal clusters 4-6.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: C. pubescens bulk RNA-seq and snRNA-seq: PRJNA1347772
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as part of coexpression-guided candidate selection for downstream enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DCE acts with DCS to produce corynantheal-type intermediates. 2. Its expression pattern matches the cinchona alkaloid biosynthetic module. 3. It is required for heterologous production of downstream cinchona intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal and catalytic roles of cinchona alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be stacked with OMT1 and core cinchona pathway genes to access methoxylated alkaloids such as quinine/quinidine/dihydro congeners in engineered hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates tryptamine at the 5-position to produce 5-hydroxytryptamine precursor for methoxylated cinchona alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces the oxygen functionality required for the methoxy group found in quinine/quinidine-type congeners.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior study; included in heterologous reconstruction where adding T5H with OMT1 generated methoxylated products.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies modified precursor pool for methoxylated target products.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: N. benthamiana transient expression with tryptamine and secologanin feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Previously identified pathway gene; detailed pattern not reported in provided text.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Previous study referenced; no accession specifically tied here beyond current transcriptome resources PRJNA1347772
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not newly discovered in this paper; establishes the parallel route to methoxylated alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 hydroxylase (inferred from function; exact family not stated here)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T5H provides the hydroxylated tryptamine precursor for methoxylated cinchona alkaloids. 2. Combined with OMT1 and the core scaffold-forming enzymes, it enabled production of methoxylated quinoline intermediates in N. benthamiana. 3. This supports a parallel biosynthetic route shared by methoxylated and non-methoxylated congeners.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Pair with T5H and core pathway genes to produce methoxylated cinchona alkaloids; useful for pathway diversification in heterologous hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates 5-hydroxytryptamine-derived precursor to generate 5-methoxytryptamine feeding methoxylated alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Creates the methoxy-bearing indole precursor leading to quinine/quinidine-type products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior study; used in N. benthamiana pathway reconstruction with T5H and core genes.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies modified precursor pool for methoxylated target alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with precursor feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Current paper resources: PRJNA1347772; enzyme discovery from previous study referenced
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports the same downstream enzymes acting on methoxylated and non-methoxylated series.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OMT1 installs the methoxy functionality on the indole precursor for quinine-type alkaloids. 2. Reconstruction experiments confirmed downstream pathway tolerance to methoxylated intermediates. 3. OMT1 with T5H enables a parallel route to methoxylated cinchona alkaloids.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CpMAT is a key scaffold-forming tailoring enzyme for pathway engineering. Stack with MCC, CiS, CiO and upstream genes; because malonyl-corynantheol is unstable, engineering should minimize intermediate buildup and ensure immediate downstream conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a malonyl group from malonyl-CoA to corynantheol to form O-malonyl-corynantheol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates corynantheol for downstream intramolecular cyclization, enabling quinuclidine ring formation and cinchonium production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with corynantheol feeding; in vitro assay with malonyl-CoA; MS/MS and NMR characterization of product; VIGS in C. pubescens causing accumulation of corynantheol congeners.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an activated immediate precursor required for scaffold cyclization toward target products.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate feeding in N. benthamiana; VIGS in C. pubescens leaves; malonyl-corynantheol hydrolyses in protic solvent and in tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with STR, DCS and DCE in bulk RNA-seq; enriched in epidermal cell clusters 4-6 in leaf snRNA-seq.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: C. pubescens bulk RNA-seq and snRNA-seq: PRJNA1347772; proteomics: PXD068683
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows in vitro acetyltransferase promiscuity but this was not observed in planta. Product 14 was not detected in native tissue because it is likely transient and unstable.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Intermediate instability may reduce yield if downstream enzymes are limiting; misbalanced expression may lead to corynantheol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for activation before cyclization; bottleneck potential supported but formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpMAT malonylates corynantheol to form malonyl-corynantheol, an activated and transient pathway intermediate. 2. VIGS of CpMAT causes strong accumulation of corynantheol congeners in planta, supporting its physiological role. 3. CpMAT initiates the unusual sequence leading to cinchonium and quinuclidine ring formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: quinine; quinidine; cinchonidine; cinchonine and dihydro congeners
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimalarial, antiarrhythmic and chiral catalyst activities

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CpMCC is essential for quinuclidine scaffold formation and should be tightly coexpressed with CpMAT to capture unstable malonyl-corynantheol. Valuable for reconstruction of natural and halogenated cinchona alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses unusual cyclization of malonyl-corynantheol, displacing malonate and generating the quaternary ammonium cinchonium.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Creates the quinuclidine ring and previously unknown cinchonium intermediate, enabling downstream cinchona alkaloid scaffold formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated candidate selection from snRNA-seq, proteomics and cross-species transcriptomics; transient coexpression with MAT in N. benthamiana produced cinchonium; activity inferred from protein extracts and feeding studies with labeled malonyl-corynantheol.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms a unique core scaffold intermediate on the route to target alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Activity seen in C. pubescens root, leaf and apoplastic protein extracts; supported by cross-species activity in Ophiorrhiza mungos and Mitragyna speciosa; transient expression in N. benthamiana with 11 feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with MAT; enriched in epidermal clusters 4-6 of leaf snRNA-seq.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: C. pubescens bulk RNA-seq and snRNA-seq: PRJNA1347772; O. mungos RNA-seq: PRJNA1413194; M. speciosa RNA-seq: PRJNA1244102; proteomics: PXD068683
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unexpectedly annotated as a BAHD malonyltransferase despite cyclase function. Conservation of related activity across Rubiaceae helped prioritize candidates.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: If not coexpressed with MAT, unstable intermediate 14 may hydrolyze and reduce flux.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD-like acyltransferase family protein with cyclase function
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpMCC converts malonyl-corynantheol into cinchonium through an unusual cyclization. 2. This step establishes the quinuclidine core central to cinchona alkaloids. 3. Discovery was enabled by combining snRNA-seq, proteomics and comparative transcriptomics.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: quinine; quinidine; cinchonidine; cinchonine and dihydro congeners
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimalarial, antiarrhythmic and chiral catalyst activities

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CpCiS is required to convert cinchonium to cinchonaminal and should be coexpressed with CpCiR to avoid overoxidation; valuable for complete scaffold reconstruction in plants or microbes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinchonium to produce cinchonaminal; can further overoxidize cinchonaminal to cinchonaminic acid when not intercepted.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces cinchonaminal, the next established intermediate toward quinoline alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate ranking by snRNA-seq coexpression; transient expression in N. benthamiana; in vitro enzymatic assays; MS/MS comparison with standard.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for quinoline-forming branch.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient N. benthamiana assays with cinchonium feeding; overoxidation occurs when CiR is absent.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enriched in epidermal clusters 4-6 together with upstream pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: C. pubescens bulk RNA-seq and snRNA-seq: PRJNA1347772
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Produces cinchonaminic acid (15) as an overoxidation product in heterologous assays, but this compound was not observed in Cinchona, implying rapid metabolic channeling/interception in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without downstream interception by CiR or later enzymes, overoxidation may waste flux into non-native cinchonaminic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Cinchonaminal (13/cyclic form of 7); overoxidation product cinchonaminic acid (15)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; important control point because of competing overoxidation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpCiS hydroxylates cinchonium to cinchonaminal, establishing the next known intermediate. 2. In the absence of interception it overoxidizes cinchonaminal to cinchonaminic acid. 3. Coexpression with other pathway genes and heterologous assays strongly support its role in cinchona alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: quinine; quinidine; cinchonidine; cinchonine and dihydro congeners
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimalarial, antiarrhythmic and chiral catalyst activities

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CpCiR can be used to divert flux from cinchonaminal to cinchonamine and suppress overoxidation by CpCiS; useful for balancing pathway flux and possibly intermediate stabilization in engineered systems.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts cinchonaminal into cinchonamine and prevents its overoxidation in the heterologous system.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate reductase screening based on coexpression with CiS; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; MS/MS comparison with standard.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a downstream branch intermediate associated with natural cinchona alkaloid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: N. benthamiana transient assays with cinchonium or cinchonaminal feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enriched in leaf epidermal clusters 4-6 with upstream genes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: C. pubescens bulk RNA-seq and snRNA-seq: PRJNA1347772
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper places cinchonamine as a reductive product from cinchonaminal; its precise contribution relative to direct quinoline formation remains a pathway nuance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert flux away from quinoline ketone formation depending on pathway balance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpCiR reduces cinchonaminal to cinchonamine. 2. Coexpression with CpCiS prevents overoxidation of cinchonaminal in heterologous assays. 3. The enzyme defines a reductive branch/parallel fate for the cinchonaminal intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cinchonamine-associated branch and cinchona alkaloid pathway intermediates

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CpCiO is indispensable for quinoline ring formation, a defining late-stage transformation. It should be included in all engineering designs targeting quinoline cinchona alkaloids and paired with a compatible downstream reductase.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes cinchonaminal, promoting oxidative indole opening/ring expansion to produce the ketone quinoline isomers cinchonidinone and cinchoninone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates the quinoline scaffold characteristic of cinchona alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression-guided candidate selection; transient expression in N. benthamiana; in vitro assays using yeast microsomes; VIGS in C. pubescens showing accumulation of upstream intermediates and reduction of quinoline ketones.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate ketone precursors of terminal cinchona alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Higher expression in root and stem tissues; VIGS in C. pubescens leaves; N. benthamiana and yeast microsome assays.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in stem and root; also enriched in leaf epidermal clusters 4-6.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: C. pubescens bulk RNA-seq and snRNA-seq: PRJNA1347772
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A CYP71 family P450. Produces two tautomeric/stereoisomeric ketone quinolines (8 and 9) in a ratio similar to the plant. VIGS provided strong in planta support.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: If downstream reductase is limiting, quinoline ketones may accumulate.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key late-stage control step establishing the quinoline scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Microsome/endoplasmic reticulum (supported by yeast microsome assays and P450 nature)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpCiO catalyses the indole-to-quinoline transformation central to cinchona alkaloid biosynthesis. 2. It generates cinchonidinone and cinchoninone in ratios resembling those in the plant. 3. VIGS knockdown causes accumulation of upstream intermediates and loss of quinoline ketones, validating its in planta role.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: quinine; quinidine; cinchonidine; cinchonine and dihydro congeners
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cinchona stem and root particularly for late steps; also leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimalarial, antiarrhythmic and chiral catalyst activities

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CpKR4 is a promising terminal ketoreductase for production of final cinchona alcohol alkaloids, but deployment requires hosts lacking inhibitory factors such as those present in N. benthamiana or engineering of enzyme/host compatibility. Stereoselectivity should be optimized, potentially by searching for alternative reductases or protein engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces ketone quinoline intermediates cinchonidinone/cinchoninone and methoxylated analogues to the corresponding alcohol cinchona alkaloids; also reduces corynantheal aldehyde substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms terminal alcohol alkaloids from ketone quinoline intermediates in vitro, linking quinoline ketones to cinchonidine/cinchonine/quinine/quinidine congeners.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Screening of 60 coexpressed NADPH-dependent reductase candidates; in vitro assays with purified recombinant protein; inhibition assays with N. benthamiana extracts.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Terminal reductive step producing target alkaloids from immediate ketone precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Activity robust in vitro but abolished by N. benthamiana aqueous extract, indicating host-derived inhibition in heterologous assays.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Selected and highly expressed in stem and root tissues; also enriched in leaf epidermal clusters 4-6.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CiO; KR1; KR2; KR3; KR5; KR6; KR7; KR8
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: C. pubescens bulk RNA-seq and snRNA-seq: PRJNA1347772
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: KR4 lacks absolute stereoselectivity; with methoxylated analogues it produced a stereoisomer not observed in planta. Authors note a more stereoselective reductase may remain unidentified.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Host inhibition in N. benthamiana prevents effective in planta use; imperfect stereoselectivity may yield non-native isomers.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Cinchonidine/cinchonine and methoxylated corresponding alcohol alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aldo-keto reductase / alcohol dehydrogenase-like NADPH-dependent reductase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant, but not proven rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpKR4 reduces quinoline ketone intermediates to final cinchona alcohol alkaloids in vitro. 2. Its activity is inhibited by N. benthamiana extracts, explaining failure in the heterologous plant host. 3. CpKR4 shows incomplete stereoselectivity, suggesting that a more selective terminal reductase may remain to be discovered.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cinchonidine; cinchonine; quinine; quinidine and dihydro congeners
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cinchona tissues, especially stem/root for late biosynthetic expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimalarial, antiarrhythmic and chiral catalyst activities

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter not specified
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for improving heterologous reconstruction efficiency by vacuolar handling of strictosidine and related intermediates when stacking upstream entry genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates pathway operation in heterologous reconstruction by transporting strictosidine, supporting production of methoxylated and non-methoxylated cinchona alkaloid intermediates.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional characterization; inclusion with STR, T5H and OMT1 in N. benthamiana reconstitution improved pathway performance from tryptamine/secologanin substrates.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive transporter component that helps maintain flux through the early monoterpene indole alkaloid pathway, but not presented as the main decisive regulator of cinchona alkaloid levels.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with precursor feeding.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Known upstream alkaloid biosynthetic gene enriched in epidermal clusters 4-6 in snRNA-seq.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transport/sequestration of strictosidine in early alkaloid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: C. pubescens bulk RNA-seq and snRNA-seq: PRJNA1347772
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transporter is not a canonical transcriptional regulator but functions as a pathway regulator by controlling intermediate compartmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Misexpression may alter compartmental balance of reactive intermediates; no explicit penalty reported.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Strictosidine transport/compartmentation rather than gene targets
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: No direct gene regulation reported; supports metabolic flux through strictosidine handling.
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuolar membrane/tonoplast (inferred from strictosidine transporter function)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STTr supports strictosidine handling in the early cinchona alkaloid pathway. 2. Its inclusion in heterologous stacks helped reconstruct methoxylated and non-methoxylated pathway flux from simple precursors. 3. It functions as a transport-based regulator of metabolite compartmentation rather than as a transcription factor.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal and catalytic roles of cinchona alkaloids
